generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component for real-time environmental temperature monitoring integrated into the Building Control System (BCS) and synchronized with the Digital Twin via BIM configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component for real-time occupancy detection integrated into the Building Control System (BCS) and synchronized with the Digital Twin via BIM configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component for real-time structural integrity monitoring integrated into the Building Control System (BCS) and synchronized with the Digital Twin via BIM configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component for real-time HVAC system performance monitoring integrated into the Building Control System (BCS) and synchronized with the Digital Twin via BIM configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component for real-time energy consumption monitoring integrated into the Building Control System (BCS) and synchronized with the Digital Twin via Digital Twin via BIM configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

instance HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric model derived from Building Information Modeling (BIM) serving as the foundational digital representation of the physical building."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance BCSBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for Building Control Systems (BCS) components including sensors, actuators, and controllers, configured by Building Control Engineers within BIM."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic operational data including sensor readings, environmental metrics, and device states synchronized in real-time between physical and digital systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted, HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance BuildingPerformanceSimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data generated from dynamic simulations of building performance under varying conditions, used for analysis and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono used for real-time data exchange between the Physical Twin (building) and Digital Twin, ensuring low-latency synchronization of sensor data and operational metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, RealTimeSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto used for real-time data exchange and model updates between the Physical Twin (building) and Digital Twin, enabling bidirectional synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSynchronizationService, CustomServiceDevelopmentService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine leveraged for rich, interactive visualization of the Digital Twin, enabling immersive 3D representations for stakeholders to explore building performance and simulate scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService, ScenarioSimulationService
]

instance BIMIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM Tools used for static model integration, where Building Information Modeling (BIM) data serves as the foundational digital representation of the physical asset."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, AutomatedArtifactGenerationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling continuous observation of the building's operational state including structural integrity, environmental conditions, and occupancy metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeOperationalInsight
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring bidirectional synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin, maintaining operational fidelity through low-latency data exchange."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationValidationInsight
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing rich, interactive visualization of the Digital Twin for immersive monitoring and control interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualBuildingPerformanceInsight
]

instance ScenarioSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting dynamic simulation of building performance under varying conditions to identify bottlenecks or optimization opportunities."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationOptimizationInsight
]

instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for defining BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM, enriching the model with semantic metadata."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationInsight
]

instance AutomatedArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automatically generating deployable Digital Twin artifacts (simulation models, control logic, data pipelines) from BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArtifactValidationInsight
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling DT Engineers to develop custom services and plugins using standardized extension points for modular integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomServiceValidationInsight
]

instance LifeCycleOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating holistic optimization across the building's lifecycle including cost reduction, energy efficiency improvements, and adaptive refurbishment planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance CollaborativeWorkflowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enhancing multi-disciplinary collaboration by offering a unified digital environment for shared decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides CollaborationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing up-to-date operational metrics such as sensor readings, actuator states, and system performance derived from real-time data synchronization."
]

instance SynchronizationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating the quality and fidelity of real-time synchronization between physical and digital systems."
]

instance VisualBuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight enabling immersive visualization of building operations in context, including spatial layouts and system hierarchies."
]

instance SimulationOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying bottlenecks or optimization opportunities through dynamic simulation of building performance."
]

instance DeviceConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating the configuration of BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within the BIM environment."
]

instance ArtifactValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating automatically generated Digital Twin artifacts for correctness and interoperability."
]

instance CustomServiceValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight validating custom services and plugins for performance and interoperability within the Digital Twin framework."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing actionable recommendations for maintenance, upgrades, or operational adjustments based on predictive analytics."
]

instance CollaborationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight facilitating multi-disciplinary collaboration through a unified digital environment for shared decision-making."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for HVAC system control, configured by Building Control Engineers within the BIM environment and synchronized with the Digital Twin for operational control."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for lighting system control, configured by Building Control Engineers within the BIM environment and synchronized with the Digital Twin for operational control."
]

instance StructuralAdjustmentActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for structural adjustment mechanisms, configured by Building Control Engineers within the BIM environment and synchronized with the Digital Twin for operational control."
]

instance EnergyManagementActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for energy management system control, configured by Building Control Engineers within the BIM environment and synchronized with the Digital Twin for operational control."
]

// C17: Actions
instance HVACControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for remote control of HVAC systems through the Digital Twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LightingControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for remote control of lighting systems through the Digital Twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance StructuralAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for remote control of structural adjustment mechanisms through the Digital Twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for automated energy optimization based on predictive analytics and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for automated predictive maintenance recommendations based on sensor data and simulation outcomes."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **bidirectional control flows** from the virtual model to physical building infrastructure, primarily facilitated by **Facility Managers** who interact with the deployed DT for real-time monitoring and operational adjustments. Control commands (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting automation, or system reconfigurations) are transmitted via the **DT Framework**, which acts as a central hub for **runtime configuration management**. While the document does not specify **command types** (e.g., setpoint adjustments, binary on/off signals) or **message formats** (e.g., JSON, XML), the system supports **dynamic adjustments without full redeployment**, implying a flexible command structure. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the **bidirectional communication** and **runtime adaptability** suggest embedded safeguards for command integrity. Feedback systems are implied through the **real-time synchronization** loop, but **conflict resolution** (e.g., priority-based arbitration, rollback protocols) and **execution confirmation** (e.g., acknowledgment tokens, status callbacks) are not detailed. The lack of explicit protocols (e.g., REST APIs, WebSockets) or security measures (e.g., TLS, OAuth) is noted, but the framework’s **modular architecture** allows for integration of such mechanisms as custom services."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical building components and their digital counterparts, enabling instantaneous bidirectional updates. The framework supports **dynamic operational needs** through **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments to synchronization parameters without full redeployment. While explicit latency requirements or granularity specifications are not provided, the emphasis on real-time synchronization implies sub-second or near-real-time synchronization frequencies for critical monitoring and control services. The system is designed to handle temporal alignment across **individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, ensuring scalability in temporal handling for heterogeneous operational contexts (e.g., sensor data, control signals, and analytics outputs)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, service-oriented constellation architecture** centered on the **DT Framework** as a unifying hub. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  - **Modular architecture** with **standardized extension points** for plug-and-play integration of custom services (e.g., analytics, IoT sensors), enabling dynamic composition of the DT’s functional stack.
  - **Runtime configuration management** allows on-the-fly adjustments to component interactions (e.g., re-routing data flows, activating/deactivating services) without full redeployment, ensuring adaptability to evolving operational requirements.
  - **Role-based coordination**: The framework orchestrates interactions between *Building Control Engineers* (BIM-based device modeling), *Facility Managers* (real-time monitoring/control), and *DT Engineers* (service extensions) via predefined workflows (e.g., deployment pipelines, data enrichment processes).
  - **Resource management** is implicit in the **scalability** non-functional requirement (NF_X), supporting expansion from individual components to entire infrastructure systems while maintaining low-overhead updates. The constellation leverages **bidirectional data exchange** to synchronize physical and digital states, with the DT Framework acting as a **mediator** for inter-component communication."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within the BIM environment, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework applies **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling automated generation of deployable DT artifacts with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time data exchange** pipeline between the physical building and its digital twin. This ensures continuous synchronization of system states.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the twin to monitor system states and operate physical infrastructure, while **interactive visualization** provides immersive interfaces for real-time oversight.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs. This modular architecture supports **runtime extensibility** and dynamic adaptation.
  Transition management between phases is governed by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless handoffs between stakeholders (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and alignment with the DT’s operational lifecycle. Governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **use-case-driven requirements** (cf. Table II) and stakeholder-specific roles (cf. Table I)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework prioritizes **model fidelity** through **semantic enrichment of BIM**, ensuring consistency between geometric, behavioral, and operational representations. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported via the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, used to evaluate the **entanglement** (i.e., synchronization and alignment) of the resulting DT with its physical counterpart. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated through **automated artifact generation** (via MDE) and **real-time data exchange**, reducing manual errors. **Error detection** is enabled by the **modular architecture**, allowing custom services to validate data integrity, though specific **quantification methods or correction mechanisms** are not detailed. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **life-cycle deployment process**, from initial device modeling to runtime operation, ensuring adherence to stakeholder requirements (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with a **central hub** (the DT Framework) facilitating bidirectional data flows between physical and digital components. The framework integrates **custom services and analytics** via standardized extension points, enabling interoperability across heterogeneous systems. While the document does not specify exact communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA, REST/gRPC), it emphasizes **real-time data exchange** and **interoperability**, suggesting support for industry-standard protocols. Network topology is not detailed, but the modular design implies a **distributed architecture** with potential edge or cloud components. Bandwidth and latency requirements are not quantified, though the focus on real-time synchronization implies low-latency, high-bandwidth needs. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are implied through the framework’s **scalability** and **maintainability** features, but no specific protocols (e.g., TLS/SSL, CityGML) or redundancy strategies (e.g., failover nodes) are mentioned."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed using a **modular, service-oriented framework** that supports **enriched BIM models** as the foundational data source. Deployment is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools, with **Facility Managers** and **DT Engineers** interacting with the deployed system for monitoring, control, and service extension, respectively. The framework’s **runtime configuration management** enables dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, suggesting support for **scalable, low-overhead updates**. While the document does not specify exact hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise), the emphasis on **interoperability**, **scalability**, and **accessibility** implies flexibility in deployment models. Computational and storage requirements are not detailed, but the system’s adaptability suggests support for **modular scaling** (e.g., adding services or components as needed). Availability and reliability are addressed through the framework’s **maintainability** features, though no explicit mention of containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes), auto-scaling policies, or multi-modal deployment strategies is provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, addressing integration challenges through **standardized extension points** for custom service deployment. While specific protocols (e.g., IFC, COBie) or external system dependencies (e.g., ERP, CMMS) are not mentioned, the system is designed to bridge **static BIM representations** with **dynamic operational needs** in the AECO domain. Integration patterns emphasize **bidirectional data exchange** between the Digital Twin and physical building systems, though the exact interoperability standards or enterprise system dependencies remain unspecified in the provided context."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **industry-standard practices** and **interoperability principles** to address the **absence of standardized integration frameworks** in current DT deployments:
  - **BIM as a Standard Foundation**: Leverages **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** as the **de facto standard** for initial device modeling, aligning with its widespread adoption in the construction industry.
  - **Standardized Extension Points**: Provides a **modular architecture** with **predefined extension points** for integrating custom services, ensuring compatibility with third-party tools and future-proofing the DT ecosystem.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Supports **real-time data exchange** via **Eclipse Hono and Ditto**, which are **open-source, standardized protocols** for IoT and edge computing, ensuring seamless integration with heterogeneous physical systems.
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: While not explicitly detailed, the framework’s **MDE-driven approach** and **BIM alignment** inherently comply with **ISO 19650** (BIM standards) and **IEC 62424** (building automation), though explicit certification requirements are not mentioned in the document.
  - **Addressing Fragmentation**: The proposal explicitly targets the **gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements**, aiming to create a **standardized integration framework** that unifies disparate tools and methodologies across the DT lifecycle."
]
